title: Governor’s remit is to invite TVK chief Vijay and ask for a floor test: former Law Minister Ashwani Kumar
content: The due process argument attributed to the Governor of Tamil Nadu is seen as a graceless stratagem on the part of the Governor to delay Vijay’s swearing-in despite a deafening popular mandate in his favour, he says

title: Kerala election results: Congress high command to pick Kerala CM; most UDF allies for Satheesan
content: A three-way fight is on in Kerala for the Chief Minister’s chair; outgoing Leader of Opposition V.D. Satheesan, Alappuzha MP K.C. Venugopal, and senior leader Ramesh Chennithala are in the race

title: Gurugram gated societies not letting in enumerators; FIRs recommended against enumerators obstructing Census work
content: Census Act, 1948 has provision for three years in jail for those found neglecting or obstructing Census duties; Gurugram Census officer writes to RWAs directing entry of Census workers with ID, authorisation letters

title: ‘No space, too old’: Four from Calvary Temple arrested for abandoning Siberian Huskies in Hyderabad outskirts
content: The Sangareddy police have written to GHMC seeking details regarding the licence held by the Calvary Temple management for dog breeding and maintenance activities.

title: Demographic imbalance in India needs more attention than population control, says RSS chief Mohan Bhagwat
content: With to Common Civil Code, Mr. Bhagwat referred to the introduction of the law in a few States in India, including Uttarakhand, and expressed optimism that the it will gradually be implemented across India

title: Gate dashing incidents at level crossings: Railways warn of criminal procedures, cancellation of driving licence
content: Every gate dash incident affects train operations and causes avoidable delays to trains, besides putting the lives of road users, railway staff, and thousands of train passengers at risk

title: Global congestion indices have risen from 20% to 25%: CAG
content: CAG is conducting a special audit of 101 Indian cities, assessing Ease of Living from the citizen’s perspective, across quality of life, access, sustainability and perception

title: Akhilesh Yadav calls on Mamata Banerjee, says Trinamool fought well
content: Votes in the West Bengal Assembly poll had been stolen by the BJP, EC and other agencies, SP leader alleges; ‘If the right to vote is taken away, then what remains of democracy?” he says

title: CPI(M), Left front launch exercise to identify shortcomings, lapses; Pinarayi Vijayan likely to be Opposition leader in Kerala Assembly
content: Leaders say party forums at various levels will listen to their immediate lower units, down to the branch, the grassroots rung of the CPI(M), as part of an intense and self-critical process of examination

title: DMK explores backing AIADMK, Tamil Nadu Governor tells Vijay he hasn’t established majority
content: The CPI(M), CPI and the VCK hold the key to any further churn in Tamil Nadu politics; in fact, their combined support is crucial for both the TVK and the AIADMK to form a government

title: India will shape the battlefield if challenged again: armed forces on Operation Sindoor
content: The operation is ‘not an end, but just the beginning’, says Lieutenant-General Rajiv Ghai; the forces destroyed 13 aircraft, either on the ground or in the air, including one high-value airborne asset at a record distance of over 300 km, says Air Marshal Awadhesh Kumar Bharti

title: Centre launches scheme for free health check-ups for workers
content: Union Labour Minister Mansukh Mandaviya, while launching the scheme, said early detection through health screenings can help prevent serious diseases, and the treatment and medicines identified during the camps will be provided through ESIC facilities

title: Despite outreach by India, Pakistan hasn’t been isolated, says Congress
content: On the contrary, Pakistan Army Chief Asim Munir has been embraced with extraordinary warmth by U.S. President Donald Trump from June 2025 onwards, says Congress leader Jairam Ramesh

title: 65% of newly elected Bengal MLAs face criminal cases; 61% are ‘crorepatis’: ADR
content: A party-wise analysis showed that 152 of 206 winning candidates (74%) from the Bharatiya Janata Party have declared criminal cases, followed by 34 out of 80 (43%) from the All India Trinamool Congress

title: SC asks if there was a 'proper debate' in Parliament before enacting CEC/EC appointments law
content: SC asks if “ethos” of its 2023 judgment was reflected in parliamentary debate on Bill returning appointment power to the political executive; lawyers note mass suspension of Opposition MPs before debate

title: Tamil Nadu Class 12 results to be declared on May 8
content: In a first, the department with the Tamil Nadu e-Governance Agency has created an avenue to view results through WhatsApp. The candidates should send a hi to 7845252525.

title: SC asks Centre to vest District Collectors with powers to enforce waste management rules
content: The rules embody efforts to protect the planet and the nation from ‘man-made destruction’, the top court says

title: Watch: DMK authorises Stalin to take decisions for a ‘stable’ T.N. govt. | Above the Fold | 07.05.2026
content: From Tamil Nadu’s post-election power struggle with the DMK’s AIADMK dilemma to Bihar’s Cabinet expansion, one year of Operation Sindoor, and a deadly virus outbreak at sea - top stories of the day.

title: Light showers likely in isolated places in A.P. until May 14, says IMD
content: Change in weather is expected due to the lower tropospheric South Easterly and South Westerly winds prevailing over the State, suggests IMD forecast

title: Flex boards appear for  leadership change in Dharmadam, Taliparamba in Kannur; CPI(M) criticises campaign
content: A flex board erected at Kurumathur endorsed the appointment of P. Jayarajan as the district secretary and M. Swaraj as the State secretary. The boards were put up in the name of ‘Kurumathur Sakhagal’

title: How ground-level social engineering changed political equation in Ponnani
content: The CPI(M) won the Ponnani municipality by a two-thirds majority even as the party suffered one of its worst defeats across Malappuram district in the recent local body elections. Despite this, KPCC secretary K.P. Noushad Ali secured a decisive Assembly victory in Ponnani

title: Tributes paid to Alluri Sitharama Raju on 102nd death anniversary
content: Officials, political parties and civil society organisations across Visakhapatnam and ASR districts mark the occasion with floral tributes

title: Telangana accounts for 20% of human trafficking cases in India
content: State accounted for nearly one-fifth of all human trafficking cases registered in the country in 2024.

title: NCRB report: Crime against elders spikes, Bengaluru has second highest crimes against women
content: Bengaluru’s crime rate stood at 117 cases per lakh population, significantly higher than Mumbai’s 74.6 and Chennai’s 25.4.

title: VIT Chennai, The Hindu Education Plus to organise webinar on visual media careers
content: The webinar aims to help students, aspiring creators with practical insights into emerging trends, career pathways, higher education opportunities, industry expectations, and importance of communication and storytelling in the digital era

title: MES to focus on healthcare sector in Kerala
content: Efforts are on to set up new hospitals in Kozhikode, Palakkad, Thrissur, Ernakulam, and Alappuzha, say Muslim Educational Society functionaries

title: NHAI plans Wayside Amenities on NH-48 in Karnataka to tackle driver fatigue, improve safety
content: These facilities are planned along strategic sections of NH 48, including the Belagavi–Kolhapur, Tumakuru–Chitradurga and Chitradurga–Hubli corridors.

title: Railway police constable accused of molesting woman beaten to death by mob
content: One of the youths, identified as Soumya Ranjan Swain, a constable with the GRP in Cuttack, was overpowered by the mob. He was tied up and severely beaten

title: Pinarayi promises to listen to people, make corrections
content: He says the Left will be a responsible Opposition, supporting the new government’s actions to improve the standard of living of the people while opposing its anti-people policies

title: Kerala Assembly polls: KC(M) faces internal turmoil ahead of steering committee meet on May 9
content: Internal strife is escalating within the Kerala Congress (M) following its failure to win any of the twelve seats it contested in the Assembly elections. Accusations of leadership failures and strained ties with churches have intensified discussions on the party’s future. Chairman Jose K. Mani downplays the crisis, emphasising that the upcoming meeting will address these pressing issues.

title: Operation Sindoor: Ten strategic lessons for India’s military future
content: Every conflict leaves behind more than tactical outcomes; it generates a blueprint for future preparedness. Operation Sindoor, when examined through a structural and doctrinal lens, reveals a decisive shift in how India must think about air power, attrition, industrial capacity, and integrated warfare. Its most enduring lessons lie not in what was achieved, but in what must now be built.

title: 'This is not Covid': WHO assures Hantavirus outbreak 'limited' but more cases 'possible'
content: The World Health Organization has assured that a hantavirus outbreak on a cruise ship is expected to be limited, though more cases are possible.  The rare Andes strain, which can spread between humans, is believed to have originated in Argentina.  Authorities confirm three passenger deaths and several infections globally, with diagnostic kits sent to affected nations.

title: Microsoft's first-ever voluntary retirement offer for 8,500-plus US employees: Here's what's in buyout package
content: Microsoft has unveiled its first voluntary retirement program, offering eligible US employees a package including five years of healthcare coverage (one fully paid), cash severance up to 39 weeks' pay, and accelerated stock vesting. This initiative aims to reduce headcount ahead of the new fiscal year, following recent layoffs and executive departures.

title: Marsh tears into RCB with century, breaks Pant's record to become ...
content: Marsh reached the milestone in 49 balls, making it the fastest century by an LSG batter in IPL history. He went past Rishabh Pant’s 54-ball hundred against RCB at the same venue in IPL 2025.

title: India’s tops global remittance charts with $137bn inflow from its 19m strong diaspora
content: India's global migration impact is soaring, with its diaspora of 19 million driving a record $137 billion in remittances for 2024. The nation also leads in student and skilled worker emigration, particularly in tech and healthcare.  Key corridors include UAE, US, and Saudi Arabia, showcasing a dynamic shift in global labor.

title: China should not have ... : Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang after share falls to zero in Beijing
content: Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang declared America a priority for advanced chip sales, stating China should not have access to the "latest and greatest" AI technology. However, he urged the US not to abandon the Chinese market entirely, arguing that global sales and increased tax revenues strengthen American economic and national security.

title: Bhuvneshwar Kumar scripts history, becomes first pacer to ...
content: Bhuvneshwar Kumar made history by becoming the first fast bowler to play 200 IPL matches, a feat he achieved with Royal Challengers Bengaluru. This milestone follows his earlier achievement of 200 IPL wickets. Kumar expressed pride in his accomplishment, highlighting the satisfaction of reaching this mark with a successful franchise.

title: From UK, BIS vaults to Indian shores: Why RBI wants to keep more & more gold at home
content: RBI is not alone in repatriating its gold reserves. Several central banks like France’s Banque de France, Germany’s Deutsche Bundesbank, Serbia’s National Bank have done the same - buy why?

title: Uber CEO admits that company is slowing hiring and the reason is AI: What he said
content: Uber is strategically slowing headcount expansion to channel more investment into artificial intelligence, according to CEO Dara Khosrowshahi.  AI is proving to be a powerful accelerator, boosting engineer productivity by generating a significant portion of code changes. This shift aims to enhance efficiency across all company operations, making the AI investment highly compelling.

title: Bengal: Governor RN Ravi dissolves Mamata cabinet; paves way for new BJP govt formation
content: West Bengal's Legislative Assembly has been dissolved by Governor R N Ravi following the BJP's decisive victory in the recent elections.  Preparations for the new government's formation are underway, with Union Home Minister Amit Shah expected in Kolkata.  Meanwhile, outgoing Chief Minister Mamata Banerjee has refused to resign, alleging widespread poll irregularities and bias.

title: 'Uri' actor Riva Arora alleges abuse by Blinkit delivery agent, Mumbai Police acts
content: Actor Riva Arora reported a distressing encounter with a Blinkit delivery agent at her Mumbai home on April 26. She alleges the agent displayed rude and inappropriate behavior, escalating to verbal harassment for nearly 40 minutes. The family contacted Mumbai Police, who responded promptly. A formal complaint has also been lodged with Blinkit.

title: Kyle Loftis death sparks questions months after major crash
content: Kyle Loftis, founder of 1320Video, died Tuesday night, according to the platform’s official statement. His cause of death has not been confirmed yet. Loftis helped build one of the biggest motorsports media brands online through street racing and automotive content. Fans, racers, and creators across social media continue sharing tributes and remembering his influence on modern car culture.

title: Simone Biles open up on therapy as Jonathan Owens eyes NFL return
content: Simone Biles opens up about her mental health struggles following the Tokyo Olympics, reflecting on the pressure that led her to step away from competition. In a recent interview, the Olympic champion shared how the experience changed her perspective on strength and vulnerability. Two years later, Biles continues to focus on her well-being while keeping her future in gymnastics open, including a possible return for the 2028 Olympics in Los Angeles.

title: Mike Vrabel’s wife Jen ‘horrified’ as Dianna Russini controversy deepens
content: Mike Vrabel is reportedly facing growing family tension after publicly admitting he is seeking counseling following controversy tied to Dianna Russini rumors. Reports claim Jen Vrabel feels “furious,” “embarrassed,” and “horrified” by the public attention surrounding the situation. The couple was recently seen together in Utah after reportedly attending family counseling sessions, though speculation about the future of their marriage continues to intensify across NFL circles and social media.

title: Tamil Nadu Class 12 results to be released tomorrow; here’s where and how to check
content: The Directorate of govt Examinations has set up a help desk for students and parents. Candidates with doubts or queries regarding the results may contact the helpline number 14417.

title: Dianna Russini, Mike Vrabel rented boat during Julio Jones trade talks
content: TMZ reports Mike Vrabel and Dianna Russini rented a private boat in June 2021 while Russini was pregnant with her first son Michael Andrew. This timing coincides with the Julio Jones trade to the Tennessee Titans from the Atlanta Falcons. This has raised questions about potential collusion. 49ers Talk host Tony Farmer suggests Dianna Russini may have influenced the trade, benefiting the Titans because of her Mike Vrabel connection.

title: HAMMER bombs, kamikaze drones: Arsenal India unleashed on Pak in Op Sindoor
content: Operation Sindoor, a year after its execution, is remembered as India's firm response to terrorism, marked by precision strikes on terror infrastructure in Pakistan. This high-tech military action showcased India's advanced arsenal, including Rafale jets, SCALP missiles, and loitering munitions, demonstrating a shift towards modern, precision warfare.

title: 'We won’t see him': Michael Clarke’s chilling warning on MS Dhoni’s future
content: Mahendra Singh Dhoni's IPL 2026 campaign is on hold due to a knee injury, with his return uncertain after a setback during training. Despite his absence, fan support remains immense, but concerns about his future and potential retirement are growing. Michael Clarke has defended Dhoni, highlighting his unparalleled influence on CSK and the sport, urging against premature retirement discussions.

title: During Op Sindoor, India destroyed 13 Pak aircraft, 11 airfields: Air Marshal Bharti
content: Indian forces successfully neutralized 13 Pakistani aircraft and struck 11 airfields during Op Sindoor, with evidence readily available.  Nine terrorist camps were also decimated on May 7.  Pakistan failed to inflict significant damage on Indian infrastructure, highlighting India's decisive response and strategic restraint in defending its sovereignty.

title: ‘Baseless imputations’: MEA dismisses Canadian spy agency’s allegations on foreign interference
content: India has rejected Canadian Security Intelligence Service (CSIS) allegations of foreign interference, calling them "baseless imputations" and reiterating its policy of not interfering in other nations' internal affairs. New Delhi also raised concerns about extremist and separatist elements operating from Canadian soil, while the CSIS report noted a more restrained tone on India compared to previous assessments.

title: How Vivek Ramaswamy tamed 'anti-Indian' MAGA and Donald Trump
content: But Vivek’s rise, if things go according to plan he might become the third Indian-origin governor of an American state after Bobby Jindal and Nikki Haley, is not just a litmus test of an Indian-American’s success story in US politics. It is a test of how far the MAGA tent will accommodate a brown non-Christian man and also how far an Indian-American can go in Trump’s America.

title: Rat poison ingredient caused deaths of four members of Mumbai family, not watermelon consumption
content: Tragedy struck a South Mumbai family as four members, including two daughters, succumbed to zinc phosphide poisoning, not watermelon as initially suspected. The family experienced severe vomiting and loose motions after consuming the chemical, commonly found in rat poison. Investigations are ongoing, with forensic reports expected to confirm the cause of death.

title: 'You put me in jail. Now, it's my turn': Messi tour organiser warns TMC
content: “Just because you have lost in the elections, don’t think that you have been punished. I will lodge a defamation case, and if needed, will go to the Supreme Court. I will fight till the end,” he wrote on Facebook stories, as quoted by Sportstar.

title: Mid-air scare: Eknath Shinde’s helicopter makes U-turn amid bad weather
content: Maharashtra deputy chief minister Eknath Shinde's helicopter returned to Mumbai due to bad weather. The aircraft, en route to Murbad for a wedding, encountered an approaching storm near Airoli. The pilot's quick thinking ensured a safe landing at Juhu, with Shinde and his staff unharmed. This incident follows a past aviation tragedy involving another deputy chief minister.

title: 'Faster repatriation of illegals': India rebuts Bangladesh ‘pushback’ claims after BJP’s Bengal win
content: India urged Bangladesh to expedite nationality verification for illegal immigrants, linking it to repatriation efforts. This response follows Bangladesh's concerns about potential "pushback" incidents after the BJP's West Bengal election victory, with Dhaka seeking cooperation for smooth deportations.

title: Australia arrests 3 women returning from Syria over alleged IS links, slavery offences
content: Australian authorities apprehended three women returning from Syria with their children, facing potential slavery and terrorism charges.  These repatriated individuals, previously held in Syrian detention camps after the Islamic State's collapse, now confront serious legal proceedings.  The arrests highlight ongoing concerns about national security and the welfare of children involved.

title: ‘TOWIE’ star Jake Hall passes away in villa party accident at age 35
content: Reality TV star Jake Hall, aged 35, tragically passed away following an accident at a villa party in Majorca. Reports indicate he sustained fatal head wounds from broken glass after allegedly smashing through a door. Authorities are investigating the incident, which witnesses suggest may have involved alcohol and agitation. The news has prompted widespread tributes from heartbroken fans and friends.

title: Yuzvendra Chahal vaping on flight? PBKS spinner takes a dig at Riyan Parag controversy
content: A viral clip from Punjab Kings’ travel vlog sparked controversy after Yuzvendra Chahal appeared to vape during a flight to Hyderabad. However, eagle-eyed fans later claimed the spinner may have been holding a spray device and merely mimicking the act. The moment, which was removed from the original vlog, has now fuelled speculation that Chahal was playfully referencing the recent Riyan Parag vaping controversy.

title: Op Sindoor anniversary: MEA says India gave ‘befitting reply’ to Pak-backed terror
content: India marked the first anniversary of Operation Sindoor, a decisive response to Pakistan-sponsored cross-border terrorism following the deadly Pahalgam attack. The Ministry of External Affairs stated the world recognized the attack and India's right to self-defense. The operation successfully targeted terror launchpads, demonstrating India's commitment to combating terrorism.

title: Kerala plus one improvement result 2026 release: Check how to download scorecards
content: Kerala Plus One improvement results for 2026 are now available on results.hse.kerala.gov.in. Students who took the improvement exams between March 5 and March 27 can access their scores by entering registration numbers and dates of birth. School authorities can also check results using school codes. The scorecard includes subject-wise marks and other crucial details.

title: 1 year of Op Sindoor: PM, his cabinet team change DP of their social media handles
content: Prime Minister Narendra Modi urged citizens to change their social media display pictures to commemorate the first anniversary of 'Operation Sindoor'. This initiative honors the Indian armed forces' valor and decisive response during the operation along the western border. The campaign aims to express respect for the forces' success and national resolve.

title: Vijay Reaches Out To New Party As Stalemate With Governor Extends Wait
content: Governor RV Arlekar remains unconvinced that the TVK can run an administration despite falling short of the required majority.

title: All 107 TVK MLAs To Resign If DMK, AIADMK Try To Form Government: Sources
content: The TVK, which won 108 seats -- two of them won by Vijay -- has 107 MLAs and argues that as the single-largest party, it should be invited by the Governor to form government.

title: 1,500 Ships Trapped In Gulf Due To Iran's Hormuz Blockade: UN Maritime Body
content: Before the conflict's outbreak, a fifth of the world's total petroleum and gas passed through the Strait of Hormuz.

title: Israeli Attack Kills Son Of Hamas Chief In Talks With Trump-Led Board
content: The attack comes as leaders of Hamas and other Palestinian factions held talks with regional mediators and the Board of Peace's lead envoy this week.

title: Congress Leader Slammed Over Video Of Him Trying To Hug Kerala Woman MLA
content: Congress leader Cherian Philip has come under severe criticism on social media after people pointed out what they called the Kerala leader's inappropriate behaviour while greeting newly elected MLA Bindu Krishna

title: Superdry Co-Founder James Holder Sentenced To 8 Years In Jail For Rape
content: A jury found Holder, 54, guilty last week of raping a woman in 2022 after meeting her in a bar in Cheltenham.

title: Man Wielding Weapon Near Ex-Prince Andrew's Home Arrested
content: Norfolk police confirmed they had been called to an incident late Wednesday near the Sandringham Estate "and the man was arrested on suspicion of a public order offence and possession of an offensive weapon".

title: Governor Dissolves Bengal Assembly After Mamata Banerjee Refuses To Resign
content: West Bengal Results 2026: Governor Dissolves West Bengal Assembly After Mamata Banerjee Refuses To Resign

title: ICE Arrests Two Indians For Attempted Lewd Act On Child, $60,000 Theft Case
content: According to the agency, Hemant Khann-Kumar, described as a "criminal illegal alien" from India, had been convicted for an attempted lewd act upon a child. Patel, meanwhile, was for conspiracy to commit theft involving more than $60,000.

title: Delhi High Court Cites Freedom Of Speech, Declines Anil Ambani's Bid For Immediate Restraint On NDTV Articles
content: The hounourable court observed that restricting publication requires detailed consideration of Article 19(1)(a) protections.

title: On 1 Year Of Op Sindoor, Documentary Shows Space-Based Assets, Cyber Ops Before Strikes
content: Operation Sindoor first anniversary: The documentary is concise, yet highly detailed with tactical information from the operation that became the Indian military's most expansive multi-domain combat mission

title: Tamil Nadu Election Results Live Updates: TVK Reaches Out To AMMK, DMK Says Lack Of Majority Complicated
content: Tamil Nadu Election Results 2026 Live Updates: The TVK emerged as the single-largest party with 108 seats in the 234-member assembly, falling short of the majority mark of 118.

title: Eknath Shinde's Chopper Turns Back To Mumbai Mid-Flight Due To Bad Weather
content: Shinde had taken off from Mahalaxmi Racecourse in south Mumbai at 3.30 pm to Thane to attend a wedding in the family of a party worker.

title: Marco Rubio Meets Pope Leo In Bid To Ease Trump-Vatican Tensions
content: The Trump administration's relations with the Holy See have sharply deteriorated since the election of the first US pope, Leo XIV, on May 8, 2025.

title: Tamil Nadu Congress Calls Statewide Protest Over Government Formation Delay
content: Chennai, May 7 (IANS) Escalating the political confrontation over government formation in Tamil Nadu, the Tamil Nadu Congress Committee on Thursday announced statewide protests on May 8 against the Bharatiya Janata Party-led Union government and Gove

title: IndiGo To Launch Inaugural Flights From Noida Airport From June 15
content: New Delhi, May 7 (IANS) Low-cost airline IndiGo announced on Thursday it will begin operations from Noida International Airport (NIA) in Jewar, Uttar Pradesh, from June 15, 2026.

title: Offered To Be "Platonic" Sperm Donor: Shivon Zilis On Relationship With Musk
content: Shivon Zilis said that it first started as a friendship after which Elon Musk reached out to her as a "platonic" sperm donor. In 2020, she decided to have children with Musk after he told her that he would "be happy to make a donation".
